title: clypeola jonthlaspi proles suffrutescens
intro: Clypeola jonthlaspi proles suffrutescens, commonly known as  White Goosefoot, is a plant species belonging to the Amaranthaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of White Goosefoot include its potential to reduce inflammation, lower blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The therapeutic actions of White Goosefoot include its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant, and its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
side_effects: Clypeola jonthlaspi proles suffrutescens can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues. Ingesting large quantities of the plant may lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's essential oils can cause respiratory problems, including coughing and shortness of breath. Additionally, improper use of the plant may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, leading to adverse reactions.
